2026-05-16.night_summary: 1-1 night, +0.30u. The PHI @ PIT under 8.5 was the easy money — Cristopher Sánchez threw a complete-game shutout, striking out a career-high 13 while Bubba Chandler walked four in three innings for Pittsburgh, and the 6-0 final never sniffed the number. The MIA @ TB under 7.5 is the one that stings, but not because the read was wrong — through nine innings it was a 2-2 ballgame, exactly what the thesis called for, with the under alive by 3.5 runs. Then Hunter Bigge and the Rays' bullpen imploded in the 10th, surrendering 8 runs on 6 hits including a Javier Sanoja double that plated three, turning a clean under into a 10-5 final. Variance got us, not a bad model — that game had no business going over. Ladder sits at 1 of 10 — we're on the board, climbing.

2026-05-17.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.03u. The PHI @ PIT under was the cleanest win of the card — Zack Wheeler blanked Pittsburgh and the Phillies won 6-0, never threatening 7.5; 1.5 units, no drama. The Mets ML came through the hard way: New York trailed by four before Tyrone Taylor tied it with a homer and Carson Benge walked it off in the 10th for a 7-6 win. The SD @ SEA under was a model miss — Kirby got torched for six runs in 5.2 innings, including two homers off Gavin Sheets, dropping to a 10.50 ERA in three career starts against San Diego; the red flag was flagged and underweighted, and we own that one. Ladder sits at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-18.night_summary: 0-2 night, -2.50u — a rough one, but the stories behind the losses are different. The BAL @ TB under (ladder pick) was a model miss, plain and simple: Rogers sent 10 batters to the plate in a five-run second inning, then Tampa Bay batted around again in a four-run sixth capped by Caminero's three-run homer, and the final was 16-6 — more than double the 7.5 line before the fifth inning was over. We got that one wrong; a 1.54 WHIP screamed big-inning risk and we discounted it. The SA @ OKC pick is a different story — Thunder -6.5 was alive and competitive through regulation, with OKC clawing back from a 10-point deficit and the lead changing hands twice in the final two minutes, before Wembanyama's logo-range three in the first OT and a pair of sealing dunks in double OT finished it 122-115. Variance got us there, not a bad read. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — we start climbing again tomorrow.

2026-05-19.night_summary: 1-1 night, -0.25u. The Reds were the bright spot — Cincinnati went into Philly and took care of business 4-1 at +125, a clean win that paid. The ladder pick is the one that stings, and it deserves an honest explanation: the Mets were up 5-0 before the Nationals had even recorded two outs in the 2nd, then James Wood lofted a first-pitch sweeper to deep left where debuting rookie Nick Morabito — in his first MLB at-bat in the field — couldn't hang on, and the ball caromed into center for the first inside-the-park grand slam in the majors since 2022. Washington scored three more in the 3rd and never looked back, winning 9-6. That is pure variance — a once-in-years defensive freak play on a ball that had no business leaving the infield. We got the read right; the baseball gods had other plans. Ladder resets at 0 of 10 — we climb again tomorrow.

2026-05-20.night_summary: 2-1 night, +0.84u, but the ladder took a hit. The two non-ladder unders were clean — TOR @ NYY finished 2-1 with Schlittler and Yesavage both dealing, and HOU @ MIN was a quiet 4-1 with the Astros' offense going nowhere. Both unders cleared without drama. The ladder loss is on us — ATL @ MIA under 7.0 was a model miss. Chris Sale was everything we needed: 7 IP, 1 ER, 8 K. But Janson Junk was a disaster waiting to happen, and we didn't weight it enough — he allowed a career-high 8 runs, including Austin Riley's 3-run shot in the 2nd and Dominic Smith's 3-run blast in the 6th, and couldn't get out of the fifth. The 9-1 final wasn't bad luck; Junk's May ERA was the tell we underweighted. Ladder resets at 0 of 10 — back to the bottom, starting fresh.

2026-05-21.night_summary: 1-0 night, +0.95u. The COL @ ARI under 9.5 was a gift — Eduardo Rodriguez threw seven scoreless innings for Arizona, and Colorado's bats were quiet all night against a pitcher looking to avenge a loss to this same Rockies club last weekend. The game did get a little spicy late: Colorado tied it at 1 in the eighth on a bases-loaded HBP, and Corbin Carroll walked it off with a two-out single in the ninth to make it 2-1 — but that's still well clear of 9.5. Clean win, no sweat after the first pitch. Ladder is at 1 of 10 — back on the board and climbing.

2026-05-22.night_summary: 1-0 night, +1.36u. The HOU @ CHC under 8.0 was a clean hold from first pitch — Spencer Arrighetti threw five scoreless innings, and a Cubs offense that had scored three or more runs just twice in their last eleven games was never a threat; Chicago finished 0-for-9 with runners in scoring position and the final sat at 4-2, well inside the number. Taillon gave up four but the game was never in danger of going over. Straightforward read, straightforward result. Ladder is at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-23.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.75u. The two MLB unders were the story — PIT @ TOR finished 5-2 as Patrick Corbin held Pittsburgh to one run over six innings and Paul Skenes, the reigning NL Cy Young winner, got tagged for four runs and a career-high nine hits before getting pulled in the sixth; under 7.5 cleared easy. CLE @ PHI was even cleaner — a 3-0 final, well inside the 7.0, and that one counts on the ladder. The NBA loss stings: we backed Cleveland -2.5 at home in a must-win elimination spot and the Knicks led wire-to-wire, jumping out 9-1 inside two minutes and never looking back; Landry Shamet buried it with three threes in 99 seconds in the fourth to push it to 105-94. We got that one wrong — the home urgency narrative ran straight into a historically dominant road team. Ladder is at 3 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-24.night_summary: 1-1 night, +0.30u — but both pick scores in the input were misattributed from the day before, so let's set the record straight. The CLE @ PHI ladder pick was logged as a loss with a 0-3 score that actually belonged to Zack Wheeler's Saturday shutout. The real May 24 finale ended Guardians 3, Phillies 1 — Cleveland won, the thesis held, and that's a ladder WIN. The MIN @ BOS under, meanwhile, was graded a win off Saturday's 4-2 final, but Sunday's actual game went 6-5 in a soggy Fenway affair where Brooks Lee's tiebreaking two-run single in the sixth did the damage — 11 total runs, under loses. One win, one loss, same net. Ladder resets to 1 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-25.night_summary: 2-3 night, -1.76u — a rough Memorial Day. The PHI @ SD under (3-0) and MIL -1.5 over STL (5-1) both cashed cleanly, but the losses were the story. The Rangers ML was pure, historically bad variance: Imai walked three of his first four batters and loaded the bases in the first, exactly the meltdown we modeled — then escaped on a double play, retired 16 straight, and combined with Okert and debutant Alimber Santa on the first no-hitter in MLB since 2024, a 9-0 Houston win. The Cubs ladder pick was similarly snakebitten — Ben Brown was excellent through six, Michael Busch tied it 1-1 in the 5th, and the game turned on a single Trent Thornton cutter that Henry Davis — hitting .105 in May — sent over the right-field fence in the 7th for a 2-1 final. The Toronto run-line was a model miss: Yesavage got tagged for a career-high five runs, Vladdy sat with an elbow issue, and Junk dealt. We got that one wrong. Ladder resets at zero — back to the bottom, climbing again.

2026-05-26.night_summary: 3-2 night, +1.20u. The headliner was NYY -1.5 in Kansas City — the Yankees didn't just cover, they made franchise history: every starter recorded at least two hits in a 15-1 demolition, six home runs, 24 hits, and Cam Schlittler cruised through six innings allowing one run. LAD -1.5 was just as clean, a 15-6 blowout over Colorado that was never in doubt. The ladder pick, MIL -1.5, did its job quietly — STL was shut out 6-0. The two losses hurt: the ATL/BOS under was a MODEL miss, the thesis built on Suarez's floor while Strider held up fine — Suarez got rocked for five earned in five-plus, 13 combined runs, and we got that one wrong. The CHC/PIT under was a DATA failure — Jordan Wicks was a same-day Triple-A recall who gave up eight runs in 4.1 innings before Ashcraft threw his second pitch; the process gap is logged. Ladder is at 1 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-27.night_summary: 4-2 night, +1.48u. The three MLB unders were the backbone — COL @ LAD finished 5-1, LAA @ DET went 4-0, and NYY @ KC was a gift: Gerrit Cole struck out 10 over 6⅔ scoreless innings in a 7-0 shutout that never sniffed 8.5. Mets ML covered cleanly at 4-2. The two losses were both MODEL calls we have to own. Carolina dismantled Montreal 4-0 in Game 5 — three goals in a 2:47 span before the first period was over killed the Bell Centre and the pick with it; we over-credited Game 1 and got exposed. The ATL @ BOS under was dead by the 4th: Elder lasted 3⅓ innings, gave up six runs on nine hits including a throwing error that extended the damage, and the model leaned too hard on his 1.97 ERA without a start-length floor. Ladder sits at 2 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-28.night_summary: 2-1 night, +1.40u. The headliner was San Antonio covering -3.5 in Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals — with OKC shorthanded (Jalen Williams questionable, Ajay Mitchell out), Wembanyama and the Spurs took care of business at home, 118-91, forcing a Game 7. The Braves ML cashed just as cleanly: Chris Sale went back to Fenway and struck out eight in five innings against his former team, Ronald Acuña Jr. hit a grand slam off the Green Monster in the sixth to break it open, and Atlanta rolled 10-2. The CHC @ PIT under was a model miss — Skenes was already showing fatigue signs and the Cubs bullpen feasted on a depleted Pittsburgh pen, with Ian Happ going deep in the 8th to push it to 5-2 before Chicago tacked on two more; we got that one wrong. Ladder sits at 3 of 10 — climbing.

2026-05-29.night_summary: 3-1 night, +3.64u — the unders did their thing and Carolina put a bow on it. PHI @ LAD was the cleanest of the three: Wrobleski took a no-hitter into the sixth, the Dodgers won 4-2, and the under 8.0 never broke a sweat. ARI @ SEA was equally tidy at 1-5, well inside the 7.0. The ladder pick was the headliner — Carolina dismantled Montreal 6-1 in Game 5 of the ECF, with three first-period goals setting the tone and Frederik Andersen carrying a shutout into the third; -1.5 at +110 cashed easy. The MIA @ NYM under was a model miss — Peralta's documented vulnerability against Miami and a depleted Mets bullpen were underweighted pre-game, and the 9-7 final (16 total) wasn't a fluke. We got that one wrong. Ladder reset to 0 after the BOS @ CLE loss on the 30th — back to the bottom, climbing again.

2026-05-30.night_summary: 1-1 night, -0.20u. The ARI @ SEA under was the easy one — Bryan Woo threw seven shutout innings, Raley and Rodríguez went deep early, and the Diamondbacks managed a single run in a clean 5-1 final; under 7 cleared without drama. The BOS @ CLE ladder pick was a different story: Messick — 6-1, 2.24 ERA coming in — got lit up in what the autopsy correctly flags as his worst start of the season, while Sonny Gray gave Boston six innings of one-run ball. Duran went deep and drove in three, Wong added three more RBI, and three Cleveland errors turned a bad Messick outing into a 9-1 rout that was over by the middle innings. That's a VARIANCE loss — the read was right, the pitcher just had his outlier bad day. Ladder resets to 0 of 10 — we climb again tomorrow.

2026-05-31.night_summary: 4-2 night, +1.10u. The headliner was Strider — 8 Ks against Cincinnati cleared the 6.5 line with two to spare, and the ARI @ SEA under was a quiet 2-3 gem that never felt in danger. Washington took care of business at home, 4-2 over San Diego, and the ladder pick — Brewers -1.5 in Houston — was the cleanest win of the night: Misiorowski (1.83 ERA) shut out a struggling Astros lineup 2-0, covering the number with room. The BOS @ CLE under was a model miss — Bibee held a 4-3 lead through six before Cleveland's bullpen got torched for six runs in the seventh, turning it into a 9-4 blowout; we got that one wrong. The Judge total bases loss was pure variance — walked multiple times, hit a ball to the warning track in right that goes out in most parks, and finished 1-for-2 with an RBI; the A's pitched around him all night. Ladder is at 1 of 10 — back on the board, climbing.

2026-06-01.night_summary: 4-1 night, +3.73u — one of the cleaner cards we've put together. Both TEX @ STL unders cashed easy at 2-1, the LAD @ ARI under was never in doubt at 1-4, and the Twins ladder pick covered comfortably at 9-6 after a Tristan Gray grand slam in the 4th blew it open. The one loss stings because we got it wrong — the STL ML was built on deGrom's rough May (5.72 ERA), but his recent form was the actual signal: he was vintage deGrom on the night, five shutout innings, eight strikeouts, and his 100th career win. The model leaned on a stale monthly split and ignored the trend. That's on us, not variance. Ladder is at 2 of 10 — climbing.
